description:
You are a logistics planner for Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) assessing emergency healthcare accessibility in the East African Community. Your goal is to identify communities that are critically underserved — far from both health facilities and major roads — to prioritize mobile clinic deployment.

Using the datasets in /home/ga/GIS_Data/east_africa/:
- communities.geojson — populated places with population data
- health_facilities.geojson — hospitals and major clinics
- major_roads.geojson — primary road network

For each community, determine its distance (in km) to the nearest health facility (nearest_facility_km) and to the nearest major road (nearest_road_km). Use EPSG:32737 (WGS 84 / UTM zone 37S) for accurate distance calculations in meters.

Compute isolation_score = nearest_facility_km × nearest_road_km and classify each community's priority_class:
- "critical": nearest_facility_km > 30 AND population > 10000
- "high": isolation_score > 50
- "moderate": isolation_score > 5
- "low": otherwise

Export to:
- /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/community_accessibility.geojson — fields: name, country, population, nearest_facility_km, nearest_road_km, isolation_score, priority_class
- /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/priority_summary.csv — columns: priority_class, community_count, total_population
